data.title: Memory, storage and optical stock declines drag down chip sector
data.description:
Stocks across the semiconductor sector, ranging from memory and storage to optical names, were demonstrating weakness during Monday morning market action.

Micron Technology (MU) and Seagate Technology (STX) had both dropped 4%, Western Digital (WDC) had declined 5%, and Sandisk (SNDK) had slipped 2.5%.

Among semiconductor equipment manufacturers, Lam Research (LRCX) led the declines as it was down nearly 5%. ASML (ASML) was down 2.7%, and KLA (KLAC) had slipped 3.6%.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) had declined 2.6%. It has fallen nearly 9% over the past five trading days.

Optical companies, which are crucial to the semiconductor industry, were also dropping on Monday. Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) was leading the decline with a 14% plunge. Coherent (COHR) had sunk 8.5%, Lumentum (LITE) had dropped 7%, and Corning (GLW) was down 6.5%. Credo Technology (CRDO) had dipped 4%.

Among CPU and GPU makers, Intel (INTC) was down 3%, while Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) had both declined less than 1%.

Marvell Technology (MRVL) had dropped 5%, and Broadcom (AVGO) had slid 2.4%. Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) was down 2.7%. Its share price has declined 14% over the past month.

The drops in the chip sector stood out from the larger market, as Wall Street's major market averages mostly moved higher as investors navigated the holiday-shortened trading week with eyes focused on the Middle East conflict. The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was relatively flat, the S&P 500 (SP500) had inched up 0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) had increased by half a percent.

data.title: FTC sues Match Group over unauthorized sharing of OkCupid data
data.description:
• The Federal Trade Commission said Monday it is suing OkCupid and its affiliate Match Group Americas (MTCH), alleging that the dating app breached its terms of privacy by sharing personal information of its users with a third party.
  • A third-party data recipient had asked the company to share large datasets of OkCupid user photos and related data with it because OkCupid’s founders were financial investors in the third party.
  • OkCupid went on to provide access to nearly three million user photos as well as location and other information without placing any formal or contractual restrictions on how the information could be used.
  • The FTC said as part of the settlement, the parties will be prohibited from misrepresenting their privacy policies and could face monetary penalties for any future violations.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Highlights from Starbucks' annual meeting
data.description:
Starbucks (SBUX) disclosed results of its annual meeting held on March 25. 

The company's shareholders elected each of the eleven directors nominated by the board to serve until the 2027 annual meeting. Marissa Mayer was the board member with the highest percentage of “for” votes. The shareholders also approved a shareholder proposal requesting supermajority shareholder voting requirements be replaced with majority voting requirements.

Among the items shareholders did not approve were a proposal requesting adoption of an independent board chair policy, a proposal requesting a report on the company's apparent exclusion of detransitioning in its healthcare coverage, a proposal requesting a report on median compensation and benefits gaps as they address reproductive and gender dysphoria care, and a shareholder proposal requesting a report on the company's use of diagnostic tools created by politicized corporate partners. Shareholders also did not approve a shareholder proposal requesting a report on the risks of the Company excluding religious charities from its employee-gift match program.

At the annual meeting, CEO Brian Niccol highlighted a series of proof points that he said demonstrated that Starbucks (SBUX) is ahead of schedule in its turnaround and delivering stronger, more consistent experiences for customers and partners. "Today, you can feel the difference," he stated. "The shine is back on Starbucks, and the momentum we’ve built is showing up in the experience our customers have every day," he added.  

Regarding the coffeehouse experience, uplifts were said to be restoring warmth and comfort across coffeehouses, with more than 1,000 expected to be completed by the end of fiscal 2026.

data.title: S&P 500 falls as valuations compress despite resilient earnings outlook, Goldman says
data.description:
U.S. equities have retreated from recent highs as rising oil prices, higher Treasury yields, and geopolitical tensions weigh on valuations, even as the earnings outlook remains resilient, according to a Goldman Sachs note.

The S&P 500 has fallen roughly 9% from its January peak, with its forward price-to-earnings ratio declining to 19 times from 21 times a month ago. The compression in multiples has come despite a rise in 2026 earnings estimates, suggesting markets are adjusting to tighter financial conditions rather than a deterioration in corporate fundamentals.

Investor positioning has also turned more cautious, with Goldman’s U.S. equity sentiment indicator falling to -0.9, its lowest level since August 2025. While such levels have historically pointed to stronger forward returns, analysts said a sustained rebound would likely require clearer improvement in the macro outlook.

“The S&P 500 index is down 9.2% from its recent highs, and the P/E ratio is down 18%. That 9 percentage point difference can be explained by an earnings cycle that is not only holding up but accelerating higher,” said Fidelity’s Jurrien Timmer. 

The quote was posted by Bloomberg's Lisa Abramowicz on X. 

Goldman expects S&P 500 earnings per share to grow 12% in 2026, supported by artificial intelligence investment, though risks from prolonged energy shocks remain. Attention now turns to the first-quarter earnings season, where guidance on margins and input costs will be closely watched.

data.title: Ultragenyx gains FDA IND clearance of UX016 for GNE myopathy
data.description:
• The US FDA has cleared Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical's (RARE) Investigational New Drug application for UX016 to treat the rare inherited neuromuscular disorder GNE myopathy.
  • UX016 is a prodrug of sialic acid that would be a substrate replacement therapy for the condition, which is characterized by insufficient production of sialic acid. 
  • A phase 1/2 study of the asset is slated to start in the second half of 2026.

data.title: Global governments have low scope for fiscal offset amid the Middle East conflict – economist
data.description:
Governments have limited capacity to provide fiscal relief from the economic fallout of the ongoing Middle East conflict, according to Holger Schmieding, chief economist at Berenberg.

In an interview with CNBC, Schmieding warned that markets are pricing in several more weeks of conflict with a rising risk profile, as the situation is “more likely in the near term to get worse rather than better.”

The economist described the current market environment as one of “grave uncertainty,” with outcomes ranging from the start of a ground invasion to a potential diplomatic deal.

However, Schmieding noted that markets remain hesitant to turn completely negative because of the unpredictable nature of the Trump administration.

“It could be that a few days from now, he announces a result of negotiations,” he said.

On oil prices (CO1:COM), (CL1:COM), Schmieding characterized current levels as a “fairly reasonable market estimate” that incorporates an “adequate uncertainty premium.”

He acknowledged that prices do not reflect either the worst or best possible outcomes but emphasized the extreme volatility witnessed over the past four weeks.

The fiscal constraints facing governments mark a significant departure from conditions in 2022.

“We definitely do not have the scope for a lot of fiscal stimuli, for a lot of fiscal offset against this,” Schmieding said, pointing to elevated debt-to-GDP ratios across the G7 nations, with the exception of Germany.

Some mitigating factors are already in place, however. Schmieding highlighted that Germany’s stimulus package is beginning to unfold and will “probably mitigate the damage significantly” for both Germany and Europe more broadly.

In the U.S., the “big bad bill” is already being implemented, providing additional fiscal support.

Despite these existing measures, the scope for new government intervention remains extremely limited.

Schmieding made clear that governments “do not have a lot of scope to now add a lot, say, in terms of energy price relief for consumers or companies,” leaving economies more vulnerable to the conflict’s economic consequences.

data.title: Adecoagro jumps 8% as UBS upgrades to Buy on urea upside, Profertil acquisition
data.description:
Adecoagro (AGRO) soars more than 8% on Monday after UBS upgraded the stock to Buy from Neutral, citing potential upside from its acquisition of a 90% stake in Profertil and strengthening urea prices. The stock gained 7.83% to $15.15 during the early trading hours of the day.

The price target for the stock was raised to $16.2, which represents a 15% upside from Friday’s close.

“Following the acquisition of a net 90% interest in Profertil, the fertilizer business has become the most relevant segment for Adecoagro, accounting for about 50% of the company's EBITDA under normalized commodity prices,” analysts led by Victor Modanese said.

Meanwhile, urea prices have surged 79% year-to-date due to supply disruptions affecting both the fertilizer and its primary feedstock, natural gas, amid Middle East tensions and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, UBS noted. Additionally, Profertil has secured low-cost natural gas supply agreements through the end of 2027.

According to UBS' Europe Chemicals team's base case scenario of a 5-week disruption, urea spreads could stay elevated into the second half of 2026 and early 2027, with lower utilization rates in the Middle East keeping the market tight for longer.

“We believe the market still underestimates EBITDA and cash flow upside from fertilizers, with current valuation implying the business' EBITDA is 10% - 20% below UBS’ estimates for 2026-27. We also view sugar prices more constructively amid tighter global S&D (supply and demand),” the analysts added.

Adecoagro stock has advanced more than 88% YTD, while it's up over 50% since the beginning of March, when the U.S.-Iran conflict broke out.
